name: MS (Entrepreneurship)
oneLiner: Build a deep-tech venture and earn a research degree for it — the programme that has produced founders since 1982.
body: Launched in 1982–83, the MS (Entrepreneurship) by Research programme pioneered entrepreneurship education in India and has matured over four decades into a global benchmark. You work on a real problem — often from an IITM research lab — and convert it into a scalable startup. Your venture is your thesis; you are evaluated on milestones, not exams.

Scholars move through a structured journey: problem identification and validation, solution development and prototyping, market testing and business modelling, then scaling and venture building — with faculty supervision, accomplished founder-mentors and the full IITM ecosystem behind them.

Programme alumni hold key technology patents, many commercialised through transfers to leading organisations. Recent cohorts have founded companies in healthcare, energy, robotics, agritech and quantum communication.
eligibility: B.Tech / B.E., or a postgraduate degree (M.Com / M.A. included)
A startup proposal submitted with the application

name: PhD (Innovation & Entrepreneurship)
oneLiner: Rigorous, applied research on how innovation emerges, how startups scale, and how lab research reaches the market.
body: SIE is building a world-class research programme that generates rigorous and applied knowledge to inform practice, policy and pedagogy in the Indian deep-tech context.

Research domains span entrepreneurship (venture science, network effects, capital structure for early-stage deep-tech, venture studios) and innovation (design innovation for the creator economy, AI in innovation and manufacturing, open innovation for scaling academic R&D, innovation ecosystems and policy, lab-to-market commercialisation pathways).

Doctoral scholars work closely with faculty and industry stakeholders on critical challenges in India's evolving innovation ecosystem, with access to IIT Madras research facilities and its startup pipeline.
eligibility: Strong academic background and research interest in entrepreneurship, innovation, technology commercialisation, innovation policy, or AI & manufacturing innovation
Open to engineering, management, economics, public policy and related disciplines

cta.label: Apply via the IITM PhD portal

name: UG Founder-in-Residence
oneLiner: A funded year to build your startup full-time, right after graduation — no job offer required, no excuses needed.

facts.v: Recent IITM UG graduates, preferably Nirmaan pre-incubated

body: Starting a company while planning the rest of your life is hard. UG-FIR removes the financial and logistical constraints so recent graduates can pursue their venture with full focus.

Backed by the IITM alumni network and the Office of Alumni & Corporate Relations, fellows get a monthly stipend, campus accommodation, dedicated mentorship, targeted workshops and access to Nirmaan's infrastructure and the wider IITM innovation ecosystem.
Apply with your personal details and pitch deck when the cycle opens; results are typically announced in the first week of June.
eligibility: Recent undergraduate alumni of IIT Madras who are startup founders
Preference for teams pre-incubated at Nirmaan

name: PG Founder-in-Residence
oneLiner: Turn your thesis into a company — funded time for MS and PhD scholars to commercialise their research.

facts.v: Synopsis submitted within the past 6 months

body: The K. Ganesh and Meena Ganesh Scholarship for Entrepreneurship Development helps postgraduate scholars transform research into viable, market-ready ventures at the exact moment it matters — right after thesis submission.

The fellowship provides a monthly stipend and residence so scholars can dedicate themselves fully to venture creation, with mentorship from experienced founders, alumni networks, and Nirmaan's pre-incubation support.
Applicants must not hold HTRA or other project funding, and apply with a research-to-market plan and synopsis when the cycle opens (previously mid-April).
eligibility: Recently graduated or graduating M.S./PhD scholars of IIT Madras intending to commercialise their research
Scholars enrolled in the MS (Entrepreneurship) programme
Synopsis submitted within the past 6 months; no concurrent HTRA/project funding

name: Entrepreneurship Courses
kicker: ID-series electives · Open to IITM students
oneLiner: Structured entrepreneurship education inside your degree — from customer discovery to launching a venture.
body: SIE's course stack gives every IITM student a structured path into entrepreneurship, taught by regular and professor-of-practice faculty with real founder experience.
